title: API Server
summary: How to expose Hermes Agent as an OpenAI-compatible HTTP API server (port 8642) supporting Chat Completions, Responses API, Runs API, and Jobs API, enabling frontends like Open WebUI, LobeChat, and LibreChat to use Hermes as a full-toolset backend.

env_vars.name: API_SERVER_CORS_ORIGINS
env_vars.purpose: Comma-separated allowed browser origins for CORS

cli_commands.purpose: Start the gateway including the API server

code_examples.caption: Chat Completions request
Responses API request
Multi-user profile setup
code_examples.code:
hermes profile create alice
cat >> ~/.hermes/profiles/alice/.env <<EOF
API_SERVER_ENABLED=true
API_SERVER_PORT=8643
API_SERVER_KEY=alice-secret
EOF
hermes -p alice gateway &

gotchas: API_SERVER_KEY is REQUIRED when binding to 0.0.0.0 — the server gives full terminal access
CORS is disabled by default — set API_SERVER_CORS_ORIGINS for browser direct access
The model field in requests is cosmetic — actual model is configured server-side in config.yaml
Stored responses (for previous_response_id) max out at 100 with LRU eviction
File upload is not supported — only inline images via data: URLs or http(s) URLs
System prompts/instructions are layered on top of Hermes core prompt, not replacing it
full_text:
# API Server

The API server exposes hermes-agent as an OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoint. Any frontend that speaks the OpenAI format can connect to hermes-agent and use it as a backend.
